meta.name: Improve Your 5k
meta.subtitle: Build speed and consistency over 12 weeks
meta.description: For runners who can already cover 5km and want to get meaningfully faster. Built around one track/interval session per week, one tempo run, one easy day and a Sunday longer run. Twelve weeks of progressive overload with two deload weeks built in.
meta.coach_notes: The interval sessions are the engine of this plan. Nail those and the rest follows. The easy runs must stay genuinely easy — if your legs feel fresh enough to push on an easy day, save that energy for Thursday.

weeks.title: Base assessment week
weeks.note: Start conservatively. The goal this week is to establish your baseline and get a feel for the paces. The Tuesday session includes a 1km time trial — run this honestly but not all-out. It tells us where you actually are, not where you think you are.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — the plan starts tomorrow.

sessions.det: 2km easy warm-up · 1km time trial (honest effort, not sprint) · 3x800m @ current 5k pace + 10sec (90sec rec) · 2km cool-down · 9km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 300ml water 30min before. Light banana if hungry — the time trial works better on a settled stomach.
20–25g protein within 30 minutes. Note your time trial result — this becomes your reference point for the whole plan.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x5 · DB row 3x10 · Push-ups 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Active recovery — gentle 20min walk is ideal today.

sessions.det: 2km easy warm-up · 20min at comfortably hard effort (can say 2–3 words, not sentences) · 1km cool-down · ~8km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 300ml water. Tempo runs work best on a light stomach — eat 90min before if you need food.
20g protein. Your legs should feel pleasantly tired, not wrecked. If they feel wrecked, the tempo was too fast.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x5 · DB row 3x10 · Push-ups 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day. Your Saturday easy run should feel comfortable.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Fully conversational · This should feel almost too easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Eat 60–90 minutes before. Porridge, toast or yoghurt. 8km at easy pace doesn't need race-day preparation.
Light protein snack or meal. Hydrate well — long run tomorrow.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Conversational throughout · Negative split if feeling good in final 3km — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Eat 90 minutes before. Porridge with banana. 500ml water with breakfast.
Proper recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein and carbohydrates. This is your most important meal of the week — don't skip it.

weeks.title: First intervals
weeks.note: Your first proper interval session this week. The 400m reps should be run at a pace you could sustain for 1–2km in a race — fast but controlled. If you went out too hard on rep 1, reps 4–6 will tell you. That is useful information.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 6x400m @ 5k goal pace (90sec walk rec) · 2km CD · 8km total · Target: even splits on all 6 reps — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 300ml water. 400m reps are short enough that you don't need much fuel — just be well hydrated.
20–25g protein. Good session — six controlled 400s is a solid foundation for what comes later in the plan.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Your legs may feel the 400s today — that is normal adaptation.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 22min tempo (comfortably hard) · 1km CD · ~8.5km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 300ml water.
20g protein. Two minutes longer than last week — you should barely notice the difference.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before. Light and familiar.
Light protein. Hydrate for Sunday's long run.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Conversational · Keep effort light even if pace creeps up — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana, 90 minutes before. 500ml water.
Full protein and carbohydrate meal within 60 minutes.

weeks.title: Building volume
weeks.note: Volume ticks up slightly this week. The 800m reps on Tuesday are longer and more demanding than 400s — pace judgement in the first rep is the key skill. Start 5 seconds per km slower than you think you should. You can always run the last 2 reps harder if you have something left.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x800m @ 5k goal pace (2min jog rec) · 2km CD · 10km total · Aim for consistent splits — no positive splits

nut.d: 300ml water. 5x800m is a significant step up — be patient in rep 1.
25g protein within 30 minutes. The 800s take more out of you than 400s — prioritise recovery today.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. You may feel the 800s more than the 400s — expected and fine.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min tempo · 1km CD · ~9.5km total · 5:00–5:15/km effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 300ml water.
20g protein. 25 minutes is starting to feel like proper threshold work — well done for staying controlled.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest. Next week is a deload — push well this week.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein. Well done this week — deload coming.

sessions.det: 12km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Your longest run so far — keep it genuinely easy and enjoy the distance

nut.d: Porridge with banana, 90 minutes before. This is your longest run of the plan — treat the nutrition accordingly.
Full recovery meal. Protein + carbs + vegetables. You have earned this one.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate rest week. Volume drops by 35%. This is not a break from training — it is when training actually happens. The adaptations from weeks 1–3 consolidate during this week. Do not be tempted to run more. Come back to week 5 feeling fresh and ready to push harder.

nut.d: 500ml water. Deload week begins. Your body is rebuilding — trust the process.

sessions.det: 10min easy warm-up · 8x(30sec faster / 90sec easy) within a 10min block · 10min easy cool-down · 6km total · Nothing hard — just keep the legs moving

nut.d: 200ml water. Easy session today — no pre-run pressure.
Light protein snack. Deload week means lighter recovery needs too.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 30min easy · 6:15–6:45/km · No structure, no targets — just run and enjoy it · ~5km

nut.d: 200ml water.
Light snack. You should feel good after this — that is the point of a deload.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest.

sessions.det: 6km easy · No targets · Enjoy feeling fresh — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Normal pre-run routine. Nothing special needed for an easy 6km.
Light protein. Week 5 builds again — come back feeling ready.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:15–6:45/km · Deload long run — comfortable and relaxed

nut.d: Normal breakfast, 90 minutes before.
Normal recovery meal. You have made it through your first month. Week 5 brings new intensity — enjoy the rest today.

weeks.title: Speed phase begins
weeks.note: The second block of the plan introduces faster, shorter intervals. The 200m reps on Tuesday are run at close to 1-mile effort — significantly faster than 5k pace. This trains your legs to turn over quickly and makes 5k pace feel easier by comparison. Keep the recovery generous.

nut.d: 500ml water. Back to training after deload — you should feel noticeably fresher.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 10x200m @ mile effort (90sec walk rec) · 2km CD · 8km total · Each 200 should feel hard but not desperate — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 300ml water. 200m reps need to be fast — give yourself permission to push.
20–25g protein. The short reps are more neurologically demanding than long ones — rest well today.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 28min tempo · 1km CD · ~10.5km total · Sustained discomfort — you should want it to end but be able to continue

nut.d: 300ml water. 28 minutes is serious threshold work — fuel properly today.
25g protein. You are halfway through the plan and the sessions are getting genuinely demanding — the improvements are coming.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational throughout — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein. Good week.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Controlled and comfortable — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana, 90 minutes before.
Full recovery meal. Protein and carbohydrates.

weeks.title: Mixed intervals
weeks.note: Mixed interval session this week — a combination of short and longer reps in the same session. This trains your body to handle changes of pace, which is exactly what racing a 5k requires. Run the 400s at 5k pace and the 200s slightly faster.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 3x(400m @ 5k pace, 90sec rec, 200m @ mile pace, 2min rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 300ml water. Mixed pace sessions are demanding — arrive rested.
25g protein. Good work — changing pace within a session is a real skill and you are building it.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 30min tempo · 1km CD · ~11km total · Your longest tempo yet — stay smooth

nut.d: 300ml water.
25g protein. 30 minutes of sustained tempo is proper race preparation — next week is a deload, so push well today.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest. Deload next week — make this weekend count.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein. Long run tomorrow.

sessions.det: 12km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Easy pace throughout · Fuelling: consider a gel or banana at 7km if feeling depleted — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 500ml water. 90 minutes before.
Full meal within 60 minutes. You have completed the first 6 weeks — the plan is working.

weeks.title: Second deload
weeks.note: Second deload. Same principle as week 4 — volume drops sharply while you absorb the last three weeks of work. You are now in phase 2 of the plan, which shifts toward race-specific preparation. Rest well this week.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 20min easy · 6x100m strides at 5k pace within the run · 5km total · Just keeping the legs sharp — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 200ml water.
Light snack. Deload means lighter everything.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 5km easy · No targets · Enjoy feeling fresh — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 200ml water.
Light snack.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 6km easy · Relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Normal pre-run routine.
Light protein. Phase 2 begins next week.

sessions.det: 8km easy · Relaxed and comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal breakfast. 90 minutes before.
Normal recovery meal. Two thirds of the plan complete — you are well on your way.

weeks.title: Race-pace work begins
weeks.note: Phase 2 introduces race-pace intervals — running at the exact pace you want to race at. This is where the plan gets specific. The 1km reps on Tuesday should feel hard but achievable. If they feel easy, you have set your goal too conservatively. If they feel impossible, reassess.

nut.d: 500ml water. Back to full intensity.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 4x1km @ goal 5k pace (2.5min rec) · 2km CD · 10km total · This is your race pace — own it

nut.d: 300ml water. This is the most demanding session of the plan so far — give it the respect it deserves.
25g protein immediately. 4x1km at race pace is real race preparation. Your body knows what to do with this — let it recover.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Your legs earned a rest day after yesterday.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min tempo (slightly below race pace) · 1km CD · ~9.5km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 300ml water.
20g protein.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Easy effort throughout — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90 minutes before.
Full recovery meal. Four more weeks.

weeks.title: Peak interval week
weeks.note: The hardest interval session of the plan. 5x1km at race pace is the closest thing to a practice 5k you will do before the real thing. The goal is not to run as fast as possible — it is to run at exactly race pace, confidently and in control. If you can do that, you are ready.

nut.d: 500ml water. Peak week. Make it count.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x1km @ goal 5k pace (2.5min rec) · 2km CD · 11km total · Run rep 1 conservatively — finish stronger than you started

nut.d: 300ml water. This is your peak interval session — arrive well rested, well hydrated, and ready to perform.
25–30g protein immediately. You just completed the hardest session of the plan. That deserves proper recognition and proper recovery.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — you earned it.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 28min tempo · 1km CD · ~10.5km total — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

nut.d: 300ml water.
25g protein.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest.

sessions.det: 9km easy · Conversational · Comfortable — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein.

sessions.det: 12km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Comfortable throughout — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90 minutes before.
Full recovery meal. Three weeks to race day. You are in excellent shape.

weeks.title: Sharpening
weeks.note: Volume starts to come down as race day approaches. The intensity stays high but the volume reduces — this is the beginning of the taper. The 600m reps are faster than 5k pace and sharpen your speed without accumulating fatigue.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 6x600m @ slightly faster than 5k pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 9km total — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

nut.d: 300ml water. Sharpening sessions feel different to volume sessions — they should feel fast and powerful.
20g protein. Good week — two more weeks after this and you race.

sessions.det: Back squat 3x10 · Romanian deadlift 3x10 · Bulgarian split squat 3x10 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 20min tempo · 1km CD · 8km total · Shorter than recent weeks — use the reduction to run it well

nut.d: 300ml water.
20g protein.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x10 · DB row 3x10 · Overhead press 3x10 · Face pulls 3x15 · Core circuit 2x45s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water.

sessions.det: 8km easy · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before.
Light protein.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 6:10–6:40/km · Comfortable — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

nut.d: Normal long run breakfast.
Full recovery meal. The work is done — two weeks of taper now.

weeks.title: Race week minus one — taper
weeks.note: Taper week. The hard work is behind you — the fitness you have built over 10 weeks does not disappear in two weeks of reduced training. Your legs should start to feel surprisingly good by Thursday. That is the taper working exactly as it should.

nut.d: 500ml water. Taper begins. The work is done.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 4x400m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 6km total · Just staying sharp — nothing hard

nut.d: 200ml water. Light session — arrive fresh and leave fresh.
Light protein snack.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest. Your legs may feel unusually good — that is the taper.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Relaxed · If your legs feel great, resist the urge to go faster — save it

nut.d: 200ml water.
Light snack. Race week is next — let the legs fully freshen.

sessions.det: Hip thrust 3x12 · Nordic curl 3x5 · Lateral band walk 3x20 · Calf raise 2x20 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Comfortable and relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Normal routine. Nothing new.
Light protein. Race week next — start thinking about your plan.

sessions.det: 6km easy · Very comfortable · Arrive at race week feeling completely fresh — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal breakfast.
Normal recovery meal. One week until race day.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Everything this week serves one purpose — arriving at the start line fresh, sharp and confident. Eat well, sleep well, stay relaxed. The fitness is there. Your only job is not to leave it in training.

nut.d: 500ml water. Race week. Eat normally and sleep well.
Slightly increase carbohydrate intake this week — pasta, rice, bread. Not excessive, just prioritise carbs over fat at each meal.

sessions.det: 15min easy · 4x100m strides at race pace (full walk rec) · 5min easy · 4km total · Wake the legs up, nothing more — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 200ml water. Light shakeout — keep it easy.
Light protein snack. Good carbohydrate meal later today.
500ml water. Complete rest. Stay off your feet if you can.
High carbohydrate meals today. Pasta or rice at lunch and dinner. Avoid anything that might cause stomach issues.

sessions.det: 15min easy · 3km total · Just keeping the legs fresh and moving — nothing more

nut.d: 200ml water. Final run before race day — keep it very easy.
Light protein. Good carbohydrate meal this evening.
Pasta or rice-based meal. Something familiar. Eat by 19:00. Avoid alcohol and anything unusual.
500ml water. Complete rest. Lay out your kit today.
Normal meals. Nothing heavy. Avoid excessive fibre. Drink consistently throughout the day — 2–2.5 litres total.

sessions.det: Warm up 1.5–2km easy · 4x100m strides · Start at goal pace · Hold through 3km · Push final kilometre if you have it · Trust your training — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana, or toast with peanut butter. 400–500ml water. Only foods you have eaten before a run before — no experiments today.
150–200ml water. Small banana optional. Pin your number, prepare your chip. Go through your race plan mentally.
1.5–2km easy jog followed by 4 strides at race pace. Your legs should feel ready and sharp by the time you reach the start line.
First kilometre: goal pace, possibly 2–3 seconds slower. Hold that feeling through 3km. At 3km, assess — if you feel good, start building. Final 500m: everything you have left.
Water immediately at the finish line. 300–400ml in the first 10 minutes. Keep moving — walk for 5–10 minutes to cool down.
Banana or protein bar if available. Start the recovery process quickly.
Proper meal within 1–2 hours. Whatever you want — you have earned it. Include protein and carbohydrates alongside the celebration. Drink consistently through the afternoon.
500ml water. Gentle walk if you want to. You ran your best 5k. What is next?
